Home / LTV Calculator

LTV Calculator

Calculate Customer Lifetime Value (LTV) and LTV:CAC ratio. Compare how different churn rates impact customer value and unit economics.

$
$
$
$

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your average monthly revenue per user.
2
Enter your gross margin percentage (typically 70-85% for SaaS).
3
Enter your monthly churn rate as a percentage.
4
Optionally enter your CAC to see LTV:CAC ratio and payback period.
5
Review your LTV, customer lifetime, and unit economics health indicator.
6
Scroll down to compare 5 LTV scenarios at different churn rates.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good LTV:CAC ratio?

A 3:1 LTV:CAC ratio is the industry benchmark — meaning your customer lifetime value should be at least 3 times your acquisition cost. Below 3:1, you are spending too much to acquire customers relative to their value. Above 5:1, you may be under-investing in growth. The sweet spot is 3:1 to 5:1.

How do I calculate LTV accurately?

LTV = (Average Monthly Revenue per User × Gross Margin %) / Monthly Churn Rate. For example: $50/month × 80% margin / 3% monthly churn = $1,333. Use actual data, not estimates. Segment by customer cohort (by acquisition month or channel) for the most accurate picture.

What is a good payback period for CAC?

Aim for 12 months or less. The best SaaS companies recover CAC in 5-7 months. If your payback period exceeds 12 months, you are tying up too much cash in customer acquisition. This strains cash flow and slows your ability to reinvest in growth.

Should I include support costs in gross margin?

Yes. Gross margin should account for all costs directly tied to serving the customer: hosting, support, third-party APIs, and payment processing fees. Many founders make the mistake of using 100% margin, which dramatically overstates LTV. 70-85% is typical for SaaS.

How does churn affect LTV?

Churn is the denominator in the LTV formula, so small changes have huge impacts. Reducing monthly churn from 5% to 3% nearly doubles your LTV (from $800 to $1,333 in the example above). This is why churn reduction is almost always a better investment than acquisition optimization.

Results

Copy All
Example

💎 Customer Lifetime Value (LTV)

• Monthly Revenue per User: $50 • Gross Margin: 80.0% • Monthly Churn Rate: 3.0% • Avg Customer Lifetime: 33.3 months • Gross Profit per User/Month: $40 📊 Key Results: • Lifetime Value (LTV): $1,333 • Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC): $150 • LTV:CAC Ratio: 8.9:1 🟢 EXCELLENT: LTV:CAC of 8.9:1 is above the 3:1 benchmark. You have strong unit economics. Consider investing more in growth. • CAC Payback Period: 3.8 months
